test_cases.title: Generic error message displayed on valid email with incorrect password
preconditions: The user account with the test email exists in the system
The login form is accessible and visible
steps: Navigate to the login page
Enter a valid, registered email address in the email field
Enter an incorrect password in the password field
Submit the login form
test_cases.expected_result: A generic error message is displayed (e.g., 'Invalid credentials') that does not reference whether the email address exists or is registered

test_cases.title: Error message for invalid email does not differ from error message for valid email with wrong password
preconditions: The login form is accessible and visible
The test email address is confirmed to NOT exist in the system
steps: Navigate to the login page
Enter a non-existent email address in the email field
Enter any password in the password field
Submit the login form
Record the exact error message text displayed
Navigate to the login page again
Enter a valid, registered email address in the email field
Enter an incorrect password in the password field
Submit the login form
Record the exact error message text displayed
test_cases.expected_result: The error message displayed for the non-existent email is identical to the error message displayed for the valid email with incorrect password, preventing email enumeration

test_cases.title: Password field is cleared after a failed login attempt
preconditions: The user account with the test email exists in the system
The login form is accessible and visible
steps: Navigate to the login page
Enter a valid, registered email address in the email field
Enter an incorrect password in the password field
Submit the login form
test_cases.expected_result: After the form is submitted and the error message is displayed, the password field contains no text (is empty)

test_cases.title: Failed login attempt is recorded in the system log
preconditions: The user account with the test email exists in the system
Access to the system logs or an audit trail is available
The login form is accessible and visible
steps: Note the current timestamp
Navigate to the login page
Enter a valid, registered email address in the email field
Enter an incorrect password in the password field
Submit the login form
Access the system logs or audit trail using appropriate credentials
test_cases.expected_result: A log entry for the failed login attempt exists, containing at minimum the timestamp and the associated email address, recorded at the time the form was submitted

test_cases.title: Successful login is not affected after a prior failed attempt
preconditions: The user account with the test email exists in the system
The correct password for the account is known
The login form is accessible and visible
steps: Navigate to the login page
Enter a valid, registered email address in the email field
Enter an incorrect password in the password field
Submit the login form
Observe the cleared password field and error message
Enter the correct password in the now-empty password field
Submit the login form again
test_cases.expected_result: The user is successfully authenticated and redirected to the expected post-login destination with no residual error message displayed

test_cases.title: Email field value is retained after a failed login attempt
preconditions: The user account with the test email exists in the system
The login form is accessible and visible
steps: Navigate to the login page
Enter a valid, registered email address in the email field
Enter an incorrect password in the password field
Submit the login form
test_cases.expected_result: After the failed submission, the email field still contains the originally entered email address while only the password field has been cleared

assumptions: The system has a mechanism (logs, audit trail, or database records) that QA can access to verify failed login attempts are recorded
A 'valid email' means an email address that is registered in the system; the requirement uses 'valid' to mean syntactically correct AND existing
Account lockout or rate-limiting behavior after multiple failed attempts is governed by a separate requirement and is not tested here
The post-login redirect destination after a successful login is defined elsewhere and is considered out of scope for REQ-002
notes: The requirement does not specify the exact text or format of the generic error message, making it impossible to assert a precise string; tests verify only that the message is non-specific about email existence. The requirement does not state whether the email field should be retained or cleared after a failed attempt — test case 6 assumes retention is the intended behavior but this should be confirmed. The format and required fields of the failed-attempt log entry are not defined, leaving the logging verification test partially ambiguous. No HTTP response code or API contract is specified, so test cases are written at the UI interaction level only.
